regions.us-ports: Today, cruise operations from US ports including Charleston, Charleston is experiencing south winds at 7 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will increase to 15 knots from the south in Charleston with overcast skies and a 3 percent rain chance, while nearby ports such as Savannah and Norfolk will see similar mild conditions with light southerly winds and minimal precipitation expected. By day 3, south-southwest winds near 13 knots and a 45 percent chance of rain showers in Charleston may affect shore excursions and outdoor activities in Charleston, Savannah, and Norfolk, warranting caution for those planning port time. Beyond 48 hours, monitoring for continued south-southwest winds and increasing rain probabilities along the southeastern US coast is advised to anticipate any operational adjustments.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, an east-southeast wind at 17 knots and a 54% chance of rain showers may affect shore excursions and embarkation in Nassau, Freeport, and Bimini, where overcast skies prevail and localized wet conditions could slow port operations. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift to north-northeast at 10 knots with an 8% rain probability, resulting in mostly overcast but drier conditions in Key West, Grand Cayman, and Ocho Rios, improving visibility and easing disembarkation procedures. Beyond 48 hours, southeast winds easing to 10 knots and a 6% drizzle chance are forecast for Falmouth, Puerto Plata, and La Romana, with continued low rain impacts but persistent cloud cover affecting outdoor excursions. Crew should monitor moderate east-southeast winds today for vessel maneuvering in Nassau and surrounding ports, while passengers should plan for possible brief interruptions to outdoor activities due to scattered showers.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today in the Eastern Caribbean, San Juan is experiencing southeast winds at 5 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent rain chance, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions with minimal weather disruptions. Looking ahead to the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to increase from the southeast to east-southeast at 10 to 15 knots, with overcast skies and a 10 percent rain chance today primarily affecting San Juan and St. Thomas, while St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua will see partly cloudy skies and no rain through tomorrow. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift easterly at 16 knots with partly cloudy conditions and a 6 percent rain chance, indicating continued favorable conditions for cruise operations and port activities across all Eastern Caribbean destinations. No marine wind warnings are anticipated during this period, allowing for routine vessel maneuvering and shore excursion scheduling. Passengers and crew should remain aware of these evolving wind speeds and sky conditions when planning activities ashore.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, Cozumel is experiencing light SSE winds at 5 knots with clear skies and a 0% chance of rain, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions to proceed without weather-related delays. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, southeast winds will increase to 10-11 knots with overcast skies and drizzle expected, with rain probabilities rising to 8% in Cozumel and similar conditions likely in Costa Maya and Belize City, though no significant impacts are anticipated. On day 3, a 45% chance of drizzle and SE winds at 11 knots may affect shore excursions and port activities in Roatan and Belize City, requiring crews and passengers to prepare for possible wet conditions. Beyond 48 hours, the elevated rain chance signals a developing trend toward more persistent drizzle across the Western Caribbean, which could influence port schedules and outdoor plans in Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City. We will continue to monitor wind directions and rain probabilities closely to keep all cruise operations running safely.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, winds out of the east at 15 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent rain probability are supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions in Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena. Port operations remain normal with good visibility and manageable sea conditions across all Southern Caribbean ports. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, elevated east winds reaching 20 knots will affect Aruba and Curacao, with Aruba seeing overcast skies and a 6% chance of drizzle on day two, which does not impact port activity. Bonaire and Cartagena will experience similar wind conditions but remain dry with minimal rain probability through this period. Beyond 48 hours, expect persistent east winds near 19 knots with light drizzle increasing slightly to 11% rain chance in Aruba, while Curacao and Bonaire maintain steady winds and low rain risk, so cruise operations should remain steady with no significant weather disruptions forecast.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, winds at Barbados are steady from the east-northeast at 8 knots under overcast skies with an 18% chance of rain, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions at Barbados and nearby ports including St. Lucia and Martinique. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, east winds will increase to 12-13 knots with drizzle and rain chances rising to 25% on Day 1 and 10% on Day 2; this may affect shore excursions and embarkation procedures in Barbados, St. Lucia, and Dominica. Ports like Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten will experience similar east to east-northeast winds near 10-13 knots with low rain probabilities, presenting minimal operational impacts. Beyond 48 hours, winds are forecast to ease slightly to 10 knots from the east-northeast with drizzle and rain chances dropping to 8%, suggesting improving conditions for port calls at St. Vincent and Grenada. Crew and passengers should monitor evolving wind shifts and drizzle for any minor adjustments to tender operations across the Lesser Antilles.
regions.los-angeles: Today in the Los Angeles / San Pedro area, winds are light from the northwest at 2 knots with overcast skies and a 3% chance of rain, resulting in smooth conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions at Los Angeles, San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect west to southwest winds increasing to 13 knots and 9 knots respectively, with overcast conditions persisting and rain chances remaining low at 8% and 1%; these conditions may require minor adjustments for docking maneuvers primarily at San Pedro and Long Beach. Beyond 48 hours, winds will ease to 8 knots from the southwest with clear skies and no rain probability, offering ideal visibility and calm seas for cruise departures from all regional ports including Catalina Island. No marine wind warnings are in effect, and current conditions support normal cruise ship operations throughout the forecast period. Passengers and crew should remain aware of overcast skies today and tomorrow but can anticipate improving weather for outdoor activities by day three.
regions.ensenada: Today in the Ensenada region, winds are light from the south-southwest at 2 knots under overcast skies with only a 5% chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect west winds increasing to 12 knots with a 33% chance of rain, which may affect shore excursions and port activities primarily in Ensenada and Punta Banda. By the second day, winds shift to the west-northwest at 15 knots with overcast skies and just a 1% chance of rain, allowing improved conditions for cruise operations in all three ports. Beyond 48 hours, winds will ease to 9 knots from the west-southwest with clear skies and no rain probability, signaling favorable conditions for ongoing ship maneuvers and shore visits throughout the region. Passengers and crew should plan accordingly for moderate winds and scattered showers on day one, with steadily improving conditions thereafter.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today in Cabo San Lucas, winds are light from the northwest at 3 knots with partly cloudy skies and no chance of rain, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds from the west-northwest at 8 knots with overcast skies and 0 to 8 percent rain probability, which will keep conditions stable for embarkation and disembarkation in all three ports. By day three, winds will remain steady from the west at 8 knots with continued overcast conditions and a 10 percent chance of rain, still below the threshold to affect cruise activities in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, or La Paz. There are no developing wind or rain threats beyond 48 hours, so cruise operations and shore excursions should continue without weather-related disruptions. Please remain attentive to updates, but current forecasts indicate favorable maritime and port conditions throughout the region.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are light from the north-northeast at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 0% chance of rain, allowing smooth port operations and uninterrupted shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing westerly to northwesterly winds ranging from 9 to 11 knots with overcast skies and no rain, which will maintain stable conditions for docking and tendering at all three ports. By day three, winds will strengthen to 16 knots from the northwest under continued overcast skies and zero rain probability, potentially causing slightly choppier seas but still within safe operational limits for cruise vessels in Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Passengers and crew should anticipate steady wind increases beyond 48 hours, but no precipitation or severe weather is forecasted that would affect embarkation or disembarkation procedures. Overall, port activities will proceed without weather-related interruptions during this period.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today in Puerto Vallarta, winds are light from the northeast at 3 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect southwest winds increasing to 10-11 knots with overcast conditions on Day 1 and Day 3, and partly cloudy skies on Day 2; all days maintain a zero percent rain probability, ensuring stable conditions for shore excursions across all four ports. The consistent southwest wind direction and moderate speeds will not trigger any Small Craft Advisories, allowing cruise vessels to maintain regular schedules without delay. Beyond 48 hours, the trend of southwest winds near 11 knots with overcast skies continues, with no rain expected, so passengers and crew should anticipate continued favorable conditions for port operations and excursions throughout the Puerto Vallarta region.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, winds are west at 5 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 0% chance of rain, allowing normal embarkation and shore excursions in Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, and surrounding ports. However, a 40% chance of thunderstorms later today with southwest winds at 8 knots may affect port operations and shore excursions in Malaga, Cadiz, Gibraltar, and Ibiza, so crews should remain alert for brief delays or changes. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, south winds increase to 9 knots with fog reducing visibility in Lisbon and Gibraltar, and a 23% chance of rain poses minimal operational concern. By day three, southeast winds at 8 knots with a 56% chance of rain showers are expected to affect Barcelona, Valencia, and Palma de Mallorca, which could impact disembarkation and shore excursions. Passengers and crew should prepare for increasing rain-related operational adjustments and monitor updates as rain showers become more likely beyond 48 hours.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, skies over Naples remain overcast with light winds from the south at 2 knots and only a 3% chance of rain, so port operations and shore excursions are proceeding without weather-related disruptions. Looking ahead to the next 24-48 hours, a 48% probability of rain combined with south winds increasing to 6 knots on Day 2 may affect shore excursions and embarkation activities in Naples as well as nearby ports including Civitavecchia and Livorno. Winds remain steady from the south at 6 knots with continued rain chances of 33% on Day 3, which could also impact Genoa and La Spezia but with less certainty. Beyond 48 hours, south winds are forecast to persist near 6 knots with a diminishing rain probability, suggesting improving conditions for cruise operations in Sardinia, Corsica, and along the Dalmatian coast including Split and Dubrovnik. Passengers and crew should monitor local updates as intermittent rain and overcast skies continue to influence visibility and outdoor activities in Monaco, Nice, and Marseille.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today in Athens (Piraeus) winds are WSW at 3 knots under clear skies with zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation as well as uninterrupted shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds shifting to SSW at 8 knots with partly cloudy skies and no rain in Athens, Santorini, and Mykonos, maintaining good conditions for port operations and outdoor activities. By the second day, winds will ease slightly to S at 6 knots with overcast skies but still zero percent rain chance, affecting Rhodes, Izmir, and Haifa with stable conditions for cruise maneuvers and shore visits. Beyond 48 hours, a 5 percent rain chance appears on day three with S winds at 6 knots and rain forecasted in Athens, potentially signaling early moisture for the region but not enough to impact port or ship operations at this time. Passengers and crew should monitor conditions around Cyprus and Alexandria as well, though no significant weather changes are expected there through this period.
